---
title: "Elliott's Lovegrass (Eragrostis elliottii)"
slug: "eragrostis-elliottii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180795304/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Elliott's Lovegrass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180795304/medium.jpg" alt="Elliott's Lovegrass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/betseyboughton">
betseyboughton</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107489699">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=288051)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in sandy, sunny habitats.

## Natural history notes

A delicate, tufted grass characterized by its fine leaves and open, airy seed heads composed of many small spikelets. In Florida, it is commonly found in dry to mesic sandy habitats such as flatwoods, sandhills, and open pine woodlands. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to groundcover in fire-maintained ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/162376-Eragrostis-atrovirens)
-   [Local profile](http://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/962)
